rule_text: A trader should only trade a system when the mathematical expectancy is positive, calculated as the probability-weighted sum of win/loss outcomes.
entry_conditions: Calculate historical win probability and average win/loss ratio
Compute expectancy: E = P(win) × avg_win − P(loss) × avg_loss
Enter trades only if E > 0.15R (15% positive expectancy per trade)
exit_conditions: Exit when system expectancy falls below 0.15R after recomputing with rolling window
Suspend trading and re-evaluate if win rate drops below historical baseline
haiku_reason: The signal specifies quantifiable calculations (win probability, average win/loss ratio, expectancy formula) and concrete numerical thresholds (E > 0.15R, win rate baseline) that a programmer could implement as testable code against historical trade data.

rule_text: A trader should size positions by weighting both the probability of loss AND the expected magnitude of that loss, rejecting trades where either component is misjudged or overlooked.
entry_conditions: Probability of loss < 30% (derived from historical win rate)
Maximum loss per trade ≤ 0.5% of account equity
Risk/Reward ratio ≥ 1:3
exit_conditions: Exit at maximum loss threshold (0.5% account loss)
Exit at profit target (1.5% account gain minimum)
haiku_reason: All entry conditions (probability of loss < 30%, maximum loss ≤ 0.5% of account equity, risk/reward ratio ≥ 1:3) and exit conditions (specific loss/gain thresholds) are quantifiable metrics that can be programmatically evaluated from historical data and portfolio state.

rule_text: A trader should size positions and accept risk only when the expected value—calculated as (probability of win × size of win) minus (probability of loss × size of loss)—is positive, and when the reward/risk ratio justifies the trade relative to the win probability.
entry_conditions: Expected Value (Prob_win × Size_win) - (Prob_loss × Size_loss) > 0.15 (15% threshold)
Win probability ≥ 55%
Reward/Risk ratio ≥ 1.5:1
exit_conditions: Exit when expected value deteriorates or thesis invalidated
haiku_reason: The signal specifies three quantifiable entry conditions (EV > 0.15, Win probability ≥ 55%, Reward/Risk ≥ 1.5:1) that can be computed from trade parameters and detected programmatically, though the exit condition is vague.

rule_text: A trader must possess a statistical edge demonstrating that cumulative profits from winning trades exceed cumulative losses from losing trades over a sufficiently large sample size before deploying capital.

Win rate × average win size > Loss rate × average loss size

rule_text: Detect and trade the predictable price tendencies created by the clustered, pulsed execution pattern of large-scale buyers and the market's memory of previous inflection points.
entry_conditions: Aggressive buying spurt detected (volume spike >150% of MA volume)
Market absorption pause observed (volume drop to <60% of spike level)
Second buying spurt detected (volume spike >150% again)
Price holds above first spurt's high by minimum 0.5% (memory of inflection)
Confirmation candle closes above resistance created by previous inflection
exit_conditions: Break below the first spurt's high by 0.5%
Absorption phase extends beyond 2x average pause duration
haiku_reason: The signal has specific, quantifiable entry conditions (volume spikes >150% MA, drops to <60%, price holds >0.5% above prior high) and exit conditions (breaks below by 0.5%, pause duration >2x average) that can be programmatically detected from OHLCV data.

rule_text: A trader should initiate trades only at points where measurable buying or selling pressure imbalance exists, rather than trading patterns themselves.

Fixed stop-loss at 1% account risk

TTF closes below entry bar range
Predefined stop loss at 2x TTF average true range
haiku_reason: The signal specifies concrete entry conditions (price position relative to moving averages, swing structures, support/resistance levels, and pattern completions) and exit conditions (structural violations, TTF closes, ATR-based stops) that can be operationalized into code to detect from market data.

rule_text: A trader should identify whether a large price impulse will reverse to prior levels or develop into a trending feedback loop, and position accordingly based on early confirmation of continued directional movement.

rule_text: A trader should identify loss of momentum in a trend by observing reluctant highs, declining bar sizes, absence of large trend bars, and multiple failed breakouts above prior extremes across multiple timeframes.
entry_conditions: Higher highs made with declining magnitude (each new high exceeds prior by less than previous increment)

Three or more failed tests above the highest high of last 5 bars
Lower timeframe confirms all four signals simultaneously
exit_conditions: Exit 50% at first target: 1.5× ATR below entry
Exit 50% on retest of recent high with rejection
Hard stop: 2× ATR above last high
haiku_reason: All five entry conditions are specific and quantifiable (declining high magnitudes, bar range contraction vs 30-day MA, zero large trend bars in 8 bars, three failed tests above highest high, lower timeframe confirmation), and exit conditions use precise ATR multiples that can be programmatically detected from market data.

rule_text: A trader should initiate short positions during the markdown phase that follows a distribution phase, because panic selling by the public marks the end of the downtrend and creates a reversal opportunity.

rule_text: A trader should avoid placing stops below accumulation zones because small losses in false accumulation patterns can rapidly escalate into large losses; instead, use accumulation zone flushes as buying opportunities rather than exit triggers.
entry_conditions: Price identified within accumulation zone (sideways consolidation with low volatility)
Confirmation: Price flushes down into zone AND bounces with higher close than entry
Volume confirmation on bounce
exit_conditions: Stop: Price closes below accumulation zone low minus 1.5 ATR
Target: Price reaches upper bound of accumulation zone or previous resistance
Time stop: Exit after 20 bars if no directional movement
haiku_reason: The signal has specific, codeable entry conditions (price in low-volatility consolidation zone, flush-and-bounce pattern, volume confirmation) and quantifiable exit rules (closes below zone low minus 1.5 ATR, reaches resistance, or 20-bar time stop) that a programmer could implement.

rule_text: A trader should maintain at least two counterbalancing trade setups from different categories to improve results and avoid over-reliance on a single market structure pattern.

rule_text: A trader should match their trading strategy type (trend-following vs. mean-reversion/fading) to the current market regime, abandoning ineffective approaches in favor of those suited to the environment.

Never initiate fade trade until 252-bar validation shows 55%+ win rate

rule_text: Enter trades in the direction of an established or nascent trend by using pullbacks within that trend to position for continuation of the trend leg.
entry_conditions: Trend is established (price has made at least 3 confirmed higher highs and higher lows, or lower lows and lower highs)
Pullback retraces 50-61.8% of the prior trend leg
Price closes back in the direction of the trend after pullback
exit_conditions: Stop loss at the recent pullback low (for uptrend) or high (for downtrend)
Exit if trend structure breaks with a reversal bar or close below trend support
haiku_reason: The signal contains specific, quantifiable entry conditions (3+ confirmed higher highs/lows, 50-61.8% Fibonacci retracement, directional close) and exit conditions (pullback extremes, trend structure breaks) that can be programmatically detected from price data.

rule_text: A trader should enter trend continuation trades only when the risk/reward ratio is favorable, defined by identifying the precise price point at which the trend is conclusively violated and ensuring this point is close enough to entry to offer attractive risk/reward characteristics.
entry_conditions: Trend is confirmed in effect (price higher lows in uptrend or lower highs in downtrend)
Violation point can be defined at a recent swing low (uptrend) or swing high (downtrend)
Distance from entry to violation point ≤ 1.0% of entry price
Potential profit target ≥ 5.0× the stop-loss distance
exit_conditions: Price closes below/above the defined trend violation point
Profit target achieved (5× risk)
haiku_reason: The signal contains specific, quantifiable entry conditions (trend confirmation via price action, defined violation points at recent swings, measurable distance threshold ≤1.0%, and risk/reward ratio ≥5:1) that can be programmatically detected from market data.

rule_text: A trader should take profits consistently at or just beyond previous resistance levels in trend continuation trades, rather than holding for extended moves, because most reliable gains occur at these defined levels while most failed continuation trades provide polite exits.

rule_text: A trader should reduce or exit positions when an obvious trend continuation play becomes extremely overextended and crowded with market participants, as reversals can be dramatic when everyone attempts to exit simultaneously.
entry_conditions: Identify established trend with obvious continuation pattern
Measure crowding: percentile of open interest or positioning at top 75th percentile or higher
Confirm overextension: price beyond 2.0 standard deviations from mean
exit_conditions: Close 50% of position when both crowding AND overextension detected
Exit remaining 50% on first reversal signal (close below 20-period MA) or after 5 bars
haiku_reason: The signal contains specific, measurable entry conditions (75th percentile open interest, 2.0 standard deviations from mean, 20-period MA) and clear exit rules (50% closes at specific thresholds) that can be programmatically detected from market data.

rule_text: A trader should avoid countertrend scalps that fade overextended rallies in established trends, especially during early development stages, because they distract from larger timeframe bias and require exceptional execution speed.

Take profit at prior support/resistance level or 2:1 risk/reward minimum
haiku_reason: All entry and exit conditions are specific and quantifiable (price breaks N%, closes back inside level, volume comparison, confirmation candle), allowing a programmer to write code to detect these conditions from OHLCV market data.

rule_text: Recognize buying climaxes marked by emotional mania and exhaustion as vulnerable reversal points; a trader should prepare to fade or exit long positions when price action shows extreme buying intensity followed by the slightest pause or loss of momentum, as the last buyer has entered and the market is vulnerable to sharp sell-offs.
entry_conditions: Price has rallied for 5+ consecutive strong up days with volume expansion
Latest bar shows a pause, doji, or small up close (diminishing momentum)
Close breaks below the low of the climax bar or below a key support level
exit_conditions: Stop loss at 61.8% retracement of the climax move

rule_text: A valid uptrend requires BOTH higher pivot highs AND higher pivot lows; a valid downtrend requires BOTH lower lows AND lower highs. Incomplete structure (e.g., higher highs without higher lows) indicates weak control and reduced trend reliability.

Failure to confirm next expected pivot in sequence

rule_text: Treat support and resistance as zones rather than precise lines, and anticipate price spikes through broken levels followed by renewed rejection at the original level—account for this behavior in risk and trade management.

rule_text: A broken support or resistance level reverses its role—broken support becomes resistance and broken resistance becomes support—due to trapped traders covering positions at breakeven, creating predictable price rejection at these pivotal levels.
entry_conditions: Price broke above resistance level (confirmed by close)
Price has moved at least 2% higher after the break
Price has returned to within 0.5% of broken resistance
Price closes BELOW the broken resistance level (rejection confirmed)
Volume at rejection is >= 90th percentile of recent volume
exit_conditions: Take profit: 2% below broken resistance level
Stop loss: 1% above broken resistance level
haiku_reason: All entry conditions are specific and quantifiable (price levels, percentage movements, volume percentiles, close position relative to resistance), enabling programmatic detection from OHLCV market data.

rule_text: A broken support or resistance level reverses its function—former resistance becomes support and vice versa—and traders should anticipate price interaction (reversal or halt) at these flipped levels.

Volume on retest is lower than break volume
exit_conditions: Stop loss: 0.5 ATR below retest level
Take profit: 1.5× risk

haiku_reason: All entry conditions are specific and quantifiable (resistance break with momentum, price retraces within 0.5-1.0 ATR, candlestick close in zone, volume comparison), allowing a programmer to codify detection rules from OHLCV data.

rule_text: A trader should be skeptical of price action at support/resistance levels that appear to fail, as shallow penetrations are often traps designed to liquidate trapped traders before the true move occurs in the opposite direction.

Volume on penetration bar is below 120% of 20-bar average
Enter reversal trade only after price closes back through the level
exit_conditions: Exit if price makes new extreme beyond penetration level by 1.0% ATR
Exit if hold is broken and level re-tested with strong volume
haiku_reason: All entry and exit conditions contain specific, measurable quantitative thresholds (ATR percentages, bar counts, volume ratios) and price levels that can be programmatically detected from market data.

rule_text: A trader should not rely on reward-to-risk ratio alone to justify entries; expected value depends critically on win rate, which deteriorates when buying at support in random or weak-signal environments regardless of ratio magnitude.

Volume confirmation at support
Prior reversal pattern at this support level
exit_conditions: Stop loss at support level breach + 1 ATR
Profit target at 3x risk amount
Time-based exit after 10 bars if no directional move
haiku_reason: All entry conditions are specific and programmable: support level proximity, backtested win rate threshold (≥65%), reward-to-risk ratio (≥3:1), volume confirmation, and prior reversal pattern detection can each be coded into a trading system, and exit conditions have precise triggers (support breach + ATR, profit target multiples, bar count).

Position is taken only after price re-enters the support/resistance zone decisively

rule_text: A valid breakout that initiates an extended trend is characterized by increased volatility measured as the expansion of individual bar ranges relative to recent price action history.

Increased volume accompanies the price breakout

Volatility compression below 1.2x the 20-bar average for 3+ consecutive bars

rule_text: A trader should view slippage at breakout points as a positive signal of genuine market movement rather than purely as a cost, and should expect to pay higher prices when entering strong breakouts.

rule_text: A trader should use a partial profit-taking strategy scaled to initial risk, with heightened caution if the first profit target is not reached and consolidation occurs near the entry level, as this signals potential trend failure.

rule_text: A trader should enter pullback trades in trending markets when price retraces with reduced bar ranges and absent countertrend momentum, as these conditions indicate valid pullbacks suitable for trend continuation participation.
entry_conditions: Primary uptrend or downtrend confirmed by higher highs/lows or moving average alignment
Price pulls back into support/resistance or moving average
Average bar range during pullback is ≤40% of pre-pullback average bar range
Volume during pullback is ≤50% of average preceding volume
No lower low (in uptrend) or higher high (in downtrend) by >0.5 ATR beyond pullback start
exit_conditions: Exit if price closes below pullback low (uptrend) or above pullback high (downtrend)
Exit if countertrend momentum exceeds 1.0 ATR
Exit on trend reversal signal
haiku_reason: The signal contains five specific, quantifiable entry conditions (trend confirmation via price structure/MA, pullback location, bar range ratio ≤40%, volume ratio ≤50%, ATR-based momentum constraint) that can be programmatically detected from OHLCV data and technical indicators.

rule_text: A trader should strictly limit loss and risk exposure when buying against support (or shorting against resistance) because multiple tests without breach indicate weakening conviction, and holding through support breaks results in being trapped as a weak-hand trader.

rule_text: Avoid buying pullbacks that test support three or more times, as multiple tests with lower highs indicate weakening buyer interest and higher probability of failure.

High of the current bounce is lower than the previous bounce high
exit_conditions: Do not enter; wait for alternative setup

rule_text: A trader should discriminate between strong trending markets and overextended (reversal-prone) markets before entering impulse-retracement-impulse pattern trades, as trades in overextended environments have significantly higher failure rates.

Distance from 20-period high > 2% (not near exhaustion)
exit_conditions: ADX falls below 20 (trend weakening)
RSI crosses above 75 or below 25
Stop loss at 2% below retracement entry
haiku_reason: All entry and exit conditions are specific and quantifiable (ADX thresholds, RSI ranges, price distance percentages, bar counts) that can be coded into an automated detection system.

rule_text: A trader should enter with-trend trades at complex consolidations on the intraday timeframe when a condition that would normally eliminate pullback opportunity (buying/selling climax) is present, by validating trend integrity on a higher timeframe where the same consolidation appears simpler.

rule_text: A trader should prequalify breakout trades by focusing on patterns that tend to support successful breakouts, rather than trading all breakouts indiscriminately, because failed breakouts are more common than winning ones but can be offset by exceptional reward/risk ratios when entry conditions are selective.
entry_conditions: Price breaks support/resistance level
Breakout occurs with above-median volume confirmation
Prior consolidation pattern shows 3+ touches of the level
Breakout direction aligns with intermediate trend (20-50 period)
No divergence signals on momentum oscillator
exit_conditions: Stop loss at 1.5x breakout amplitude below entry
Take profit at 3.0x risk (high reward:risk ratio compensates for selectivity)
Exit on close below breakout level + 1 ATR
haiku_reason: All entry and exit conditions are specific and quantifiable (volume comparison, touch count, trend period, oscillator divergence, ATR-based levels) and can be programmatically detected from OHLCV data and technical indicators.

rule_text: Do not mechanically reverse a failed breakout position; instead, wait for confirmation that the breakout has genuinely failed before entering a counter-trend trade, as volatile breakouts with pullbacks that violate the level are common and lead to whipsaws.
entry_conditions: Breakout level violated by at least 1 close
Price closes below breakout level for 2+ consecutive bars
No short entry taken on initial violation
exit_conditions: Exit long at breakout level immediately (no hold)
Do not enter short position; wait for next setup
haiku_reason: The signal has specific, quantifiable entry conditions (breakout level violated by at least 1 close, 2+ consecutive closes below level, no prior short entry) and exit rules that a programmer could encode into automated detection logic using price data and trade history.

rule_text: A trader should enter trades only within defined structural patterns that have a clear risk point, rather than trading in isolation based on price extremes or support/resistance levels alone.
entry_conditions: Overextended price move identified (e.g., RSI > 80 or < 20 on primary timeframe)
Pattern structure confirmed on primary timeframe (e.g., buying climax, reversal bar, failed test)
Higher timeframe (2+ levels up) confirms directional bias or support/resistance
Risk point is clearly defined at pattern extreme
Entry occurs after pattern closes or on first pullback confirmation
exit_conditions: Stop loss placed at pattern-defined risk point (e.g., climax high for short)
Exit on break of pattern structure support

rule_text: A trader should initiate a trend-following trade when price consistently remains on one side of a moving average, and should confirm the trend direction by the slope of the moving average itself.

exit_conditions: Price closes on opposite side of MA
MA slope flattens below 0.2 pips per bar
haiku_reason: All entry and exit conditions are quantifiable (percentage of bars, MA slope direction, specific pip thresholds) and can be programmatically detected from OHLC data and moving average calculations.

rule_text: A trader should fade (reverse) moves that extend beyond established channel boundaries, as there is a statistical tendency for price to revert back through those boundaries.

Price extension is 0.5x to 1.0x the channel width
No additional confirmation required
exit_conditions: Price closes back inside the Keltner channel
Initial stop loss at 2.0x ATR from entry
Take profit at midline or opposite band
haiku_reason: The signal has specific, quantifiable entry conditions (price beyond Keltner channel boundary, extension of 0.5x-1.0x channel width) and clear exit rules that a programmer could code using standard technical indicators and price data.

rule_text: A trader should enter trades by either fading price moves that close outside volatility bands or entering pullbacks that touch the moving average after the band has been breached, capitalizing on mean-reversion behavior within defined price structure.
entry_conditions: Price closes outside Keltner Channel band (not mere touch)
OR: Price touches/crosses MA after band was previously breached, with close confirmation
Fade: Short when close above upper band; Long when close below lower band
Pullback: Long at MA after upper band touch; Short at MA after lower band touch

exit_conditions: Fade: Exit on close back inside band
Pullback: Exit on re-touch of opposite band or at 2xATR profit target

rule_text: A trader should distinguish between normal market cycles (impulse-consolidation alternation with pullbacks at extremes) and distorted cycles (where extreme strength or weakness short-circuits normal mean-reversion), and trade accordingly.
entry_conditions: Price has moved to band extremes (1.5σ to 2.0σ from mean)
Pullback into middle band zone (±0.5σ from mean) confirmed
No price acceleration or extreme volatility spike detected
Previous impulse move is <2 standard deviations
exit_conditions: Exit if price reverses back to opposite band extreme
Exit if volatility spikes above 75th percentile
Profit target at middle band ±0.25σ
haiku_reason: All entry and exit conditions are operationalized around Bollinger Bands (sigma multiples), pullback zones, volatility percentiles, and price movements that can be computed from OHLCV data.

rule_text: When price presses into and remains at an extreme band boundary (upper or lower) with no pullback, this indicates exhaustion and one-sided imbalance; professionals should exit longs (or avoid entries) and stand aside rather than chase pullbacks.
entry_conditions: Price sustains within upper or lower band for >= 8 consecutive bars
Pullback depth < 20% of prior move amplitude
No close back to midline or beyond in lookback period
exit_conditions: Close position immediately upon 8-bar band duration + low pullback confirmation
Stand aside until price recaptures midline with normal pullback behavior
haiku_reason: The signal contains three specific, measurable entry conditions (8 consecutive bars at band boundary, pullback depth <20%, no close to midline) that a programmer could codify using price data and band calculations.

rule_text: A trader should avoid trading and wait for consolidation when a market makes a sharp move in one direction immediately followed by an equally sharp reversal through the opposite band, as this environment typically leads to triangle consolidation and reduced directional clarity.
entry_conditions: Price spikes above upper Bollinger Band by >1.5 standard deviations
Price immediately reverses and spikes below lower Bollinger Band by >1.5 standard deviations within 3 bars
Consolidation pattern (ATR < 50th percentile of 20-bar ATR) is confirmed over 5+ bars before re-entry
exit_conditions: Do not enter until triangle consolidation is fully formed and volatility contracts below median
If early entry, exit on first move back through either band
haiku_reason: The signal contains specific, quantifiable entry conditions (price spikes >1.5 standard deviations through bands within 3 bars, ATR < 50th percentile over 5+ bars) and exit rules that a programmer could implement using market data and technical indicators.

Let’s turn our attention to some practical applications of the MACD, and how it might
be used in actual trading. Formally and precisely, the MACD mea
rule_text: Use MACD to identify momentum inflection points and exhaustion zones where price momentum is likely to reverse or fail to continue after consolidation.
entry_conditions: MACD histogram reverses sign after 5+ consecutive bars in one direction
Price closes beyond 2.5 standard deviations from 20-period MA
Momentum persistence threshold: MACD remains in exhaustion zone for 2+ bars
exit_conditions: Exit on momentum reversal signal reversal (MACD turns back toward exhaustion direction)
Exit on fixed profit target of 1.5x risk
Exit on time stop of 20 bars
haiku_reason: All entry conditions are quantifiable (MACD histogram sign reversal after 5+ bars, price beyond 2.5 standard deviations from 20-period MA, MACD in exhaustion zone for 2+ bars) and exit conditions are specific (MACD reversal, 1.5x risk profit target, 20-bar time stop), allowing a programmer to implement these rules in code.

rule_text: Enter a position on the pullback following a significant new momentum extreme, anticipating continuation in the direction of the initial momentum move.
entry_conditions: Momentum indicator (MACD) makes a new multi-period high or low (lookback: 50+ periods)
Price retraces 38-50% of the move that generated the momentum extreme
Momentum indicator shows divergence or flattening during pullback (not making new extreme in same direction)
Enter on resumption of trend: price breaks above/below pullback level with momentum confirmation
exit_conditions: Stop loss: below/above the pullback swing low/high by 1.5x recent ATR
Take profit: at next significant resistance/support or 2:1 risk/reward

Exit if trend resumes strength
Max hold: 2 bars after entry

rule_text: A trader should validate that momentum divergences occur between actual price swings with intermediate retracement; divergences without price retracement between measurement points are false signals and should be filtered out.
entry_conditions: Momentum indicator shows divergence pattern between two swings
Price retracement between measurement points ≥ 50% of prior swing
Retracement forms a distinct swing low/high, not just a minor pullback
No trend continuation without reversal structure
exit_conditions: Exit if price breaks beyond the divergence measurement points without reversal
Exit if retracement fails to materialize within 10 bars of divergence signal
haiku_reason: The signal specifies measurable entry conditions including momentum divergence detection, quantifiable retracement thresholds (≥50%), distinct swing identification, and a 10-bar exit window that a programmer could operationalize with market data.

PLACING THE INITIAL STOP
The placement of the initial stop is very important. First, it sets the ultimate risk point
on the trade and so removes much of the emotion from the trade process. This point
rule_text: A trader should place initial stops at a distance that reflects the trade-off between position size and win probability, using the stop distance to inform position sizing that removes emotion from trade management.
entry_conditions: Technical setup identified with clear, nearby support/resistance
Stop distance ≤ 0.5% of instrument price (tight stop)
exit_conditions: Price hits initial stop loss
Position reaches 2:1 or 3:1 reward-to-risk
haiku_reason: The signal has specific, actionable entry conditions (technical setup with nearby support/resistance + stop distance ≤ 0.5% of instrument price) and clear exit conditions (stop loss hit or 2:1/3:1 reward-to-risk) that a programmer could detect from price data and position metrics.

rule_text: A trader should use automated or fixed systems to remove emotional decision-making during real-time position management, especially at market extremes where emotional panic (capitulation at lows, desperation covering at highs) drives price.
entry_conditions: Signal generated by mechanical rule
Position size predetermined and non-negotiable
exit_conditions: Exit at pre-defined profit target (no adjustment allowed)
Exit at pre-defined stop loss (no adjustment allowed)
No mid-trade management or averaging
haiku_reason: The entry conditions specify mechanical rule generation and predetermined position sizing, while exit conditions define fixed profit targets and stop losses—all of which are objectively detectable and codifiable in an automated system.

rule_text: A trader should establish a clear pre-trade plan for position entry and scaling to reduce emotional decision-making vulnerability during execution, particularly when managing active positions under market pressure.
entry_conditions: Setup signal confirmed AND position size determined
Pre-trade plan documents entry method (scale-in vs all-at-once)
If scale-in chosen: allocate into 5 equal tranches with fixed price levels or time intervals
exit_conditions: All planned tranches filled OR market conditions violate setup thesis
haiku_reason: The signal contains specific actionable conditions that can be programmatically verified: confirmation of setup signal, documentation of position size, specification of entry method (scale-in vs all-at-once), and if scale-in is chosen, allocation into exactly 5 equal tranches with defined price levels or time intervals.

rule_text: A trader scaling into a position must actively monitor and limit cumulative risk to prevent end position size and loss from exceeding original plan.
entry_conditions: Scaling entry signal identified (e.g., mean reversion setup, buy-the-dip opportunity)
Current cumulative position size < maximum allowed
Each tranche size = 25% of max intended position
exit_conditions: Stop loss hit at original planned level
Cumulative position reaches 1.0x intended max
Exit scaling entries entirely; do not add beyond this limit
haiku_reason: The signal has specific, actionable entry conditions (scaling signal identified, cumulative position size < maximum, tranche size = 25% of max) and clear exit conditions (stop loss level, position reaches 1.0x max) that a programmer could implement as detectable triggers from market data and portfolio state.

rule_text: A trader should pre-define both an absolute dollar risk amount and an ultimate drop-dead stop level, then continuously track position size and average price to ensure the loss at that stop level never exceeds the pre-defined risk amount.
entry_conditions: Define absolute dollar risk amount = 0.5% of account
Set ultimate stop loss distance = 1.5x ATR from entry
Calculate maximum position size = risk_amount / stop_distance
Do not scale in; maintain fixed position size
exit_conditions: Exit at pre-defined ultimate stop loss level without exception
Do not move stop closer to entry after partial profits
haiku_reason: All entry conditions are specific and quantifiable (0.5% risk, 1.5x ATR calculation, position size formula), and exit conditions can be programmatically detected against price levels and stop orders.

rule_text: A trader should establish a pre-planned exit strategy for partial profit-taking rather than exiting positions impulsively, to preserve edge in winning trades and avoid the psychological bias toward locking in small profits prematurely.
entry_conditions: Trade entry with defined risk stop loss established
Written exit plan created at entry (not after entry)
Full position size deployed at entry
exit_conditions: Exit 1/3 position when trade reaches 2.0x initial risk
Exit 1/3 position when trade reaches 3.0x initial risk
Trail final 1/3 with 1.5 ATR stop or let winners run
haiku_reason: All entry and exit conditions are specific and measurable: risk stop loss establishment, written plan creation at entry, position sizing, and profit targets at defined multiples of initial risk (2.0x, 3.0x) with a trailing stop mechanism (1.5 ATR) that can be programmatically detected from market data and trade records.

rule_text: Avoid making partial exits (profit-taking or loss-reduction) at noise levels relative to average true range; only execute partial exits when price movement is material relative to the instrument's volatility.

Move stop to breakeven or trail remaining position

rule_text: A trader should take partial exits at multiple predetermined points early in a favorable trade to lock in gains and reduce risk, rather than holding the full position until a single exit point.
entry_conditions: Full position entered at initial signal
Trade moving in intended direction with confirmed momentum
exit_conditions: Exit 20% of position at first local swing high within 1.0 ATR of entry
Exit additional 20% at each subsequent swing high
Hold remaining 20% for trend continuation
haiku_reason: The rule contains specific, actionable entry conditions (full position at initial signal, confirmed momentum), quantifiable exit triggers (20% exits at swing highs within 1.0 ATR increments), and a programmer could detect local swing highs and ATR-based price levels from market data to implement this partial exit strategy.

rule_text: Avoid taking partial exits at a loss, as locking in losses anchors P&L in the loss space and makes recovery difficult; instead, exit the entire position at a small loss rather than deleveraging through partial loss exits.

Trade moves into loss territory (current price < entry price)
exit_conditions: Exit entire position when unrealized loss reaches 0.5%
Do not take any partial profits or partial exits while in loss state
haiku_reason: The signal has specific, measurable entry conditions (position established, unrealized loss exists) and quantifiable exit triggers (0.5% loss threshold) that a programmer could implement with market data and portfolio state monitoring.

rule_text: Build a profit cushion by scaling out of winning positions at predefined profit targets and re-entering at lower prices within range-bound markets, reducing ultimate loss exposure if the trade fails.

exit_conditions: Exit 50% at 1.0X profit
Stop loss on remaining 50% at original stop level
One re-entry allowed if price returns to entry zone
haiku_reason: The signal contains specific, actionable conditions that can be coded: detecting accumulation/range zones, triggering exits at 1.0X profit threshold, managing stop losses at original levels, and re-entry logic when price returns to entry zone.

rule_text: A trader should scale into extended trending positions by reducing position size at range extremes to preserve dry powder and capital for continuation moves, accepting smaller wins in ranging periods in exchange for outsized profits during multi-leg trends.
entry_conditions: Market identified in multi-leg uptrend or downtrend
Price at 75th+ percentile of recent range (top) or 25th percentile (bottom)
Position size reduced to 40% of maximum allocation at range extremes
Build remaining 60% across subsequent trend legs away from range edges
exit_conditions: Exit 50% at first profit target after range extreme
Trail stops on remaining position by 2 ATR
Exit on first reversal signal (close below/above key level)
haiku_reason: The rule contains specific, quantifiable entry conditions (75th/25th percentile thresholds, 40% position sizing) and measurable exit triggers (2 ATR trails, specific profit target levels) that can be programmatically detected from market data and portfolio state.

rule_text: A trader should maintain constant position size across multiple entries in a trend, taking partial profits at targets to lock in gains and gradually accumulate a risk-free core position, while resisting the emotional urge to increase size on later entries despite growing profits.
entry_conditions: Valid setup emerges in direction of established trend (confirmed by price action or technical indicator)
Position size has been reduced by partial profit-taking from prior entry
No more than 2 concurrent full-size entries accumulated
exit_conditions: Exit 75% at first profit target (33% gain)
Exit remaining 25% at second profit target (50% gain)
Trend confirmation broken: exit all remaining positions
haiku_reason: The rule contains specific, actionable entry conditions (valid setup in trend direction, position size reduced by prior profit-taking, maximum 2 concurrent entries) and concrete exit conditions (75% exit at 33% gain, 25% exit at 50% gain, exit on trend confirmation break) that a programmer could implement with market data and portfolio state tracking.

rule_text: A trader should scale position size in proportion to their psychological comfort threshold, not mechanically to trend momentum, because pyramiding beyond one's emotional capacity leads to poor decision-making and account ruin.

exit_conditions: Position reaches 2x multiplier cap

Trend reversal signal triggered

exit_conditions: Exit entire pyramid at first loss signal or trend break
Risk-adjusted deterioration detected in subsequent additions

rule_text: A trader should reduce or hedge correlated positions during periods of financial stress or crisis, because correlations between diverse assets converge toward 1.0 in such regimes, eliminating the diversification benefit.

rule_text: Choose execution method (limit vs market order) based on trading frequency and cost structure: high-volume short-term traders should use limit orders to capture liquidity rebates and offset transaction costs, while accepting adverse selection risk.
entry_conditions: Trading frequency >= 10 trades per day
Expected rebate income / expected transaction costs >= 0.60
Bid-ask spread < 2 ticks
Average trade hold time < 5 minutes
exit_conditions: Limit order not filled within 30 seconds
Switch to market order execution
haiku_reason: All four entry conditions are quantifiable (trading frequency count, rebate-to-cost ratio, spread measurement, hold time duration) and a programmer could detect them from market data and execution logs, and the exit condition provides a clear 30-second timer trigger.

rule_text: A trader should enter counter-trend positions on a retest of the previous trend extreme after a failed breakout, rather than chasing the initial breakout, to exploit the panic liquidation that occurs when trapped long traders exit.

rule_text: A trader should reject or significantly scrutinize a trading system when statistical testing reveals that observed performance cannot be reliably distinguished from random chance, indicated by high p-values and volatility that dwarfs mean returns.

haiku_reason: The signal specifies quantifiable statistical thresholds (p-value < 0.05, StdDev ratio < 10x) and defined data windows (closed trades, rolling 50 trades) that a programmer can implement to automatically detect entry and exit conditions.
